intent:
The zoomed-IN control for `zoomed_out_thumb_storm.toml`. Byte-for-byte the
same scenario — same library, same settle waits, same quickview open,
fullview dive and clip advance — with ONLY the four zoom-out keypresses
removed. The default grid's visible set stands in as the control.

Its whole job is to isolate the zoom-out as a variable. Read the same
instruments and diff them against the storm run:
  - `thumb_requests` — how many tier-1 requests each visible-set size
    admits. This is the flood, sized.
  - `queue_wait: thumb` mean/max — whether a visible thumb waits behind
    the pool at this visible-set size.
  - `decode reads` >100ms/>1s and `selected_served` — whether the video
    thread suffers WITHOUT the storm, i.e. whether the zoom-out is causal
    or the SMB link alone accounts for it.

Run this back-to-back with the storm scenario, never concurrently: two
runs against the same network volume contend and invalidate both.
